title: IOC on bulk RNAseq analysis
text: Training on bulk RNAseq analysis with Galaxy is upcoming!

The InforBio platform offers a series of “Short-Lectures” in Zoom (maximum 30 minutes) on tools and best practices in bioinformatics and biostatistics.
These “Short-Lectures” take place on Tuesday mornings at 9:30 a.m.
We’d love for you to join us, learn and share together, and grow with the community!

title: What is IOC?
text:
The InforBio platform has developed an “Interactive Online Companionship” (IOC) specifically tailored to initiate researchers into bioinformatics. The IOC involved one-to-many virtual work sessions where companions are trained to analyze their own data.
All IOC programs are structured into a series of modules, with each module building upon the knowledge and skills acquired in the previous one. One module lasts one week, during which companions dive into online materials to ensure they understand and internalize the content. Companions then complete quizzes and exercises using both public and their own data to gauge their understanding, receiving personalized feedback on their work. To facilitate their learning, we provide access to computational resources such as Rstudio and Galaxy servers. The module wraps up with videoconference to recap the week’s material and address any questions. These videoconferences are recorded and made available for later viewing, ensuring continuous access to the learning resources.
By the end of an IOC, companions will be equipped with expertise and confidence to run their own analyses independently.

title: 2-Day R Training
text:
The InforBio platform offers a short in-person R training course, taught in English, designed to refresh core skills and help you level up your coding. It also provides a foundation for further training, such as scRNAseq data analysis.






Participants should have some prior experience with R, as the training assumes familiarity with the basics.



The training will take place on March 12th and 13th, 2026, with the following planned content:

Quarto and good practices (including working with servers)
R essentials: data frame manipulation and path management
Descriptive analysis and data visualization with ggplot2
Principal component analysis (PCA)

The training will be limited to a maximum of 10 participants.
Registration fees:

IBPS/SU students: €150
IBPS/SU staff (researchers, postdocs, engineers, etc.): €250
External academic participants: €350
Industry/private sector participants: €600


Registration is now closed.

title: About Us
text: We provide biological researchers with statistical and bioinformatics expertise, analysis tools, and high performance computing resources. Our team covers a broad range of applications, with specific core expertise in transcriptomics and epigenetics.

title: IOC R and scRNAseq trainings
text:
Version FrançaiseEnglish Version


La plateforme InforBio vous propose des formations en bioinformatique en ligne, en petits groupes, adaptées aux besoins des laboratoires de recherche. Nous proposons un parcours complet sur 6 mois, organisé en deux modules complémentaires.
Programme de la formation

Introduction à R pour l’analyse de données (novembre 2025 à janvier 2026) – 10 sessions de 3 heures – 800 €
Devenez autonome en R : maîtriser les fondamentaux, l’organisation et filtrage de données, les analyses statistiques de base, et la création de graphiques prêts pour publication. Aucun pré-requis.

Putting It All Together: Reproducible Analysis with Quarto

Analyse de données scRNAseq (mars à juin 2026) – 10 sessions de 3 heures – 2000 €
Apprenez à analyser des données de séquençage ARN en cellules uniques grâce à des cas pratiques. Vous travaillerez d’abord sur un jeu de données fourni, puis sur vos propres données, avec un retour personnalisé sur votre projet. Cette formation requiert une bonne maîtrise de R.

Week 9

Week 10

La formation complète est proposée au tarif de 2500 €.
Points clés :

Sessions en petits groupes pour un apprentissage interactif et individualisé.
Mode hybride avec 3 sessions en présentiel et 7 à distance.
Mise en pratique avec un projet individualisé présenté à la fin de chaque formation.
Accompagnement personnalisé avec feedback sur vos propres données.
Places limitées, inscriptions déjà ouvertes.


Inscription est maintenant clôturée.
N’hésitez pas à nous contacter si vous avez des questions !



The InforBio platform offers you online training in bioinformatics, in small groups, adapted to the needs of research laboratories. We offer a complete course over 6 months, organized in two complementary modules.
Training program

Introduction to R for data analysis (November 2025 to January 2026) – 10 sessions of 3 hours – €800
This course covers the basics of R, data organization and filtering, basic statistics, and creation of publication-ready graphics. The goal is to make you self-sufficient in using R for your own analyses.

scRNAseq data analysis (March to June 2026) – 10 sessions of 3 hours – €2000
Learn how to analyze single-cell RNA sequencing data through practical examples. You’ll work on a provided dataset and receive personalized feedback on your own projects. This training requires a proficiency in R.

The complete training package is available for €2500.
Key highlights:

Small group sessions for interactive and personalized learning.
Hybrid mode with 3 in-person sessions and 7 remote sessions.
Hands-on practice with an individualized project presented at the end of each training course.
Tailored feedback on your own data.
Limited spots available, registration is now open.
